subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Print the current version

subcommands.name: enable
subcommands.description: Turn on automatic backups (required root + full disk access)

subcommands.name: disable
subcommands.description: Turn off automatic backups (required root + full disk access)

subcommands.name: startbackup
subcommands.description: Begin a backup if one is not already running

options.description: Run the backup in a mode similar to system-scheduled backups
Wait (block) until the backup is finished before exiting
Allow automatic destination rotation during backup
Perform the backup to the destination corresponding to the specified ID

subcommands.name: stopbackup
subcommands.description: Cancel a backup currently in progress

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes backups with the specified timestamp from the backup volume mounted at the given mountpoint

options.description: Delete from the given mount point
Delete backups with this timestamp
A specific path to delete (HFS backup disk)

subcommands.name: deleteinprogress
subcommands.description: Delete all in-progress backups for a machine directory

subcommands.name: restore
subcommands.description: Restore the item `source`, which is inside a backup, to the location `destination`. Same semantics as `cp`

options.description: Be verbose, showing files as they are copied

subcommands.name: compare
subcommands.description: Perform a backup diff

options.description: Compare extended attributes
Compare all supported metadata
Compare creation times
Compare file data forks
Compare ACLs
Compare file flags
Compare GIDs
Unknown
Unknown
Compare file modes
No metadata comparison
Compare sizes
Compare modification times
Compare UIDs
Don't take exclusions into account when comparing items inside volumes
Ignore logical volume identity (volume UUIDs) when directly comparing a local volume or volume store to a volume store
Print output in XML property list format
Limit traversal depth to depth levels from the beginning of iteration
Ignore paths with a path component equal to name during iteration

subcommands.name: setdestination
subcommands.description: Configure a local HFS+ or APFS volume, AFP share, or SMB share as a backup destination (requires root + Full Disk Access)

options.description: Add the arg to the list of destinations, instead of replacing the list
Enter a password in a non-echoing interactive prompt

subcommands.name: removedestination
subcommands.description: Remove the destination with the specified unique identifier from the Time Machine configuration (requires root + Full Disk Access)

subcommands.name: destinationinfo
subcommands.description: Print information about destinations currently configured for use with Time Machine

options.description: Print output in XML plist format

subcommands.name: addexclusion
subcommands.description: Configure an exclusion that tells Time Machine not to backup a file, directory, or volume during future backups

options.description: Configure fixed-path exclusions
Configure volume exclusions

subcommands.name: removeexclusion
subcommands.description: Configure  Time Machine to backup a file, directory, or volume during future backups

options.description: Configure fixed-path exclusions
Configure volume exclusions

subcommands.name: isexcluded
subcommands.description: Determine if a file, directory, or volume are excluded from Time Machine backups

subcommands.name: inheritbackup
subcommands.description: Claim a machine directory or sparsebundle for use by the current machine

subcommands.name: associatedisk
subcommands.description: Bind a volume store directory to the specified local disk, thereby reconfiguring the backup history (requires root + Full Disk Access)

options.description: Find all volume stores in the same machine directory that match the identity of the volume backup directory

subcommands.name: latestbackup
subcommands.description: List this computer's latest completed backup

options.description: Specify a destination volume to list backups from
Attempt to mount the backups and list their mounted paths
Only show the backup timestampt rather than the full name or path

subcommands.name: listbackups
subcommands.description: List all of this computer's completed backups

options.description: Specify a destination volume to list backups from
Attempt to mount the backups and list their mounted paths
Only show the backup timestampt rather than the full name or path

subcommands.name: machinedirectory
subcommands.description: Print the path to the current machine directory for this computer

subcommands.name: calculatedrift
subcommands.description: Analyze the backups in an HFS machine directory and determine the amount of change between each

subcommands.name: uniquesize
subcommands.description: Analyze the specified path in an HFS+ backup or path to an APFS backup and determine its unique size

subcommands.name: verifychecksums
subcommands.description: Compute a checksum of data contained within a backup and verify the results against checksum information computed at the time of backup

subcommands.name: localsnapshot
subcommands.description: Create new local Time Machine snapshots of all APFS volumes included in the Time Machine backup

subcommands.name: listlocalsnapshots
subcommands.description: List local Time Machine snapshots of the specified volume

subcommands.name: listlocalsnapshotdates
subcommands.description: List the creation dates of all local Time Machine snapshots

subcommands.name: deletelocalsnapshots
subcommands.description: Delete all local Time Machine snapshots on all mounted disks for the specified date or on the given disk

subcommands.name: thinlocalsnapshots
subcommands.description: Think local Time Machine snapshots for the specified volume
